task_completion.description: Open the Execute SQL Statement dialog in LibreOffice Base
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Execute SQL Statement' dialog (accessed via Tools -> SQL) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Execute the SQL command to create the CustomerAuditLog table
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CREATE TABLE command for 'CustomerAuditLog' is visible in the 'Command to execute' text area with a 'Command successfully executed' status message, OR the 'CustomerAuditLog' table appears in the Tables list of the main LibreOffice Base window.

task_completion.description: Execute the SQL command to create the insert trigger
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CREATE TRIGGER command for 'trg_customer_insert' is visible in the SQL execution window, and the status section shows 'Command successfully executed'.

task_completion.description: Execute the SQL command to create the delete trigger
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CREATE TRIGGER command for 'trg_customer_delete' is visible in the SQL execution window, and the status section shows 'Command successfully executed'.

task_completion.description: Execute the SQL command to insert the test customer record
task_completion.visual_evidence: The INSERT INTO command for the test customer (ID 60, 'Audit', 'TestUser') is visible in the SQL execution window, and the status section shows 'Command successfully executed'.

task_completion.description: Save the database file to persist changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Save' button (floppy disk icon) in the main LibreOffice Base window, selects File -> Save, or confirms saving the database when closing the application.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Base GUI to execute commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the LibreOffice Base application and its SQL dialog, rather than running terminal commands or scripts to modify the database.

integrity.description: The agent opened the correct Chinook database file
integrity.visual_evidence: The title bar of the LibreOffice Base window shows 'chinook.odb'.

integrity.description: The commands were genuinely executed by the database engine
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Command successfully executed' message is clearly visible in the LibreOffice Base SQL dialog for the executed commands, rather than just typing them into a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The database file is located at /home/ga/chinook.odb. The expected table to be created is 'CustomerAuditLog'. The expected triggers are 'trg_customer_insert' and 'trg_customer_delete'. The test customer inserted must have CustomerId=60, FirstName='Audit', LastName='TestUser'. The agent must execute the provided SQL commands in LibreOffice Base and save the database to persist changes.
